title: Department of Economics and Finance (English) - Course Content
content:
Department of Economics and Finance (English) - Course Content

1. SEMESTER

IBLG105 Information and Communication Technologies (2-0) 2, 4 AKTS

The aim of the program is to give usage abilities of operating system, internet and office programmes (MS Word, Excel and Powerpoint) by being introduced basic concepts of computer system.

IEKO103 Introduction to Economics-I  (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of the course is to teach the students the basic concept of microeconomics to enable them understand how a free market economy operates by the dynamics of supply and demand under the guiding force of market price which in return balances under the stress of third supply and demand.

IISL109 Basic Law (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of the course is to provide students with information on basic law and basic concepts of law.

IISL101 Business Administration-I (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The main purpose of this course is to ensure students have the view which is necessary for themselves to be successful in the applied fields of business environment by providing them the information related to the topic of business management as well as discussing management organization theories and contemporary approaches.

IMAT115 Mathematics-I (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to introduce the engineering students to the basic concepts of the Calculus such as limit, contiunity and differential and evolve them to an expertise level and to provide the students with the properties of analytical thinking, discussion and evaluation.

IISL105 Financial Accounting-I (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of the course is to provide students with informatin about the creation of records and statements in General accounting, explaining accounting transactions for the period and basic arrangements of the active accounts

2. SEMESTER

ITUR104 Turkish Language (4-0) 4, 4 AKTS

The aim of the course is to teach the student the place of Turkish language in world languages; its history, development, and structural features

IISL102 Business Administration-II (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide more detailed insight about business functions and their inter-relations.

IMAT104 Mathematics-II (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to educate the students use mathematics to have the capability of systematic thinking and solving of problems they will encounter in business life and the future management positions by the help of mathematics.

IEKO102 Introduction to Economics-II (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide students with a knowledge of the basic concepts and processes of economics through the help of current examples.

ISOS102 Sociology  (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

To learn the basic sociological concepts and to analyze the emerging conditions of sociology in our country and in the West. To learn the basic concepts of sociological thinking and sociological world and to have knowledge on how to utilize them in social events.

IISL108 Financial Accounting-II (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide students with information about the creation of records and statements, explaining accounting transactions for the period, short-and long-term assets and income and expenditure accounts, financial accounts and basic arrangements and reports of income and expenditure accounts.

3. SEMESTER

IYDL203 Foreign Language-I (3-0) 3, 3 AKTS

The purpose of this course is to enable the students to recognize basic grammatical structures, to use the structures they have already recognized, to gain basic vocabulary needed for A2 level, to define the structures they have already learned through reading materials and to express themselves orally and in writing, to recognize the listening materials at A2 level, and participate in oral practices and express themselves correctly at A2 level.

IATA201 Ataturk’s Principles and History of Turkish Revolution (4-0) 4, 4 AKTS

The aim of the course is to enable the students to comprehend Atatürk's principles and revolutions in the light of the challenges and struggle of the independence war. Also, it aims to teach the political developments in the process of establishment of the new state

IISL201 Law of Obligations (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide students basic informationt about he community, the relationship between the concepts of the state and the rule of law, the state and the rule of law, the law that it is a case of living in the discussions.

IISL203 Statistics (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to inform students about principles of basic statistics and give information about doing statistical analyses by using statistical package programs.

The aim of this course is to teach e-commerce approaches and practices in the context of globalization.

IIYB203 Business Ethics (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide students with the standards of professional ethics, professional responsibility, the principles of ethics, and the ability to question and assess paradoxsum of the profession.

4. SEMESTER

IYDL206 Foreign Language-II (3-0) 3, 3 AKTS

The purpose of this course is to enable the students to recognize basic grammatical structures, use the structures they have already recognized, gain basic vocabulary needed for A2 level,define the structures they have already reconized through the reading materials and express themselves orally and in writing, explain listening materials at A2 level, and participate in oral practices and express themselves correctly at A2 level.

IIYB204 Entrepreneurship (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this program is to provide the students with the approaches for critical thinking and commenting, and skills to apply these approaches to enforce creativeness and innovation in order to foster entrepreneurship

IISL206 Labor Law and Social Security (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

Labor Law and Social Security course aims to explain that trhere should be balance between employees and employer for the sake of social welfare and order. It also covers theoritical and prectical solutions for Social rights, payments and working conditions of workers

IISL208 Business Finance (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide students with basic information about the basic concepts of corporate finance.

IISL210 Quality Assurance and Quality Management Systems (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of the course is to provide the students with information about quality and quality management systems and help them acquire the prespective required in the applied fields.

IISL212  Commercial Law (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide the students with the discussions and basic information regarding the relationship between the concepts of society, law and state; supremacy of law and state and that law is a living thing.

5. SEMESTER

IYDL301 Foreign Language-III  (3-0) 3, 3 AKTS

The aim of the course is to introduce the basic concepts of Political Science and International Relations discipline.

IISL309 Human Resources Management (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide the students with the discussions and basic information regarding the relationship between the concepts of society, law and state; supremacy of law and state and that law is a living thing.

The aim of this course is to help the students realize the process of financial decision- making of companies in achieving financial goals by demonstrating the relationship between accounting and finance. The course also explains budgeting which is important in enhancing the values of companies, capital structure and financial instruments and techniques used to improve working capital management.

I8ISEC1 Elective - I (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

IISL319 - Accounting Standarts (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The aim of this course, 'International Accounting Standards' and 'International Financial Reporting Standards' to  introduce the conceptual framework. The course also be transferred to Turkey Accounting Standards and practices  related to these standards.

ILOJ321 - Supply Chain and Logistic Management (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

This course aims to teach students the basics of logistics and supply chain, to build models for solving supply chain  and logistics problems and to develop various techniques.

ISL315 - Accounting Audit (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The aim of the course is to equip students with the purpose, role and importance of audit in terms of businesses, and  society, techniques and process of audit and modern developments in the audit.

IISL317 Cost Accounting (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

Businesses Cost Accounting, methods, structure and operation process of Operations is to provide students gain them.

IISL303 Organizational Behaviour (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The aim of this course is to examine and analyze the behaviour of the individuals working in a business in terms of variants of the group, organization and enviroment.

Production/operations management involves the integration of numerous activities and processes to produce products and services in a highly competitive global environment. Many companies have experienced a decline in market share as a result of their inability to compete on the basis of product design, cost or quality. It is clear that good performance in operations, i.e., in product design, manufacturing, engineering and distribution, is essential for competitive success and long term survival.

6. SEMESTER

IYDL302 Foreign Language-IV (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of the course is to help students advance their professional English levels that they reached during YDL301.

IISL320 Management Information Systems (3-0) 3, 3 AKTS

The aim of this course is to analyse and develop an understanding of Management Information Systems which is essential for creating competitive firms, managing global corporations, adding business value and providing useful products ands services to the customers, to understand the importance of MIS for business functions.

IISL322 Administrative Accounting (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of this course is to give the information about the historical development of the public relations ,and the relationships of other disciplines and the differences in relationship.

I8ISEC2 Elective - II

IISL324 - International Business Law (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

To provide a general apprehension of business law through a presentation of basic concepts, principles and institutions of the law of obligations, commercial law, intellectual and industrial.

IISL314 - Public Relations and Communication (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The assertions/ the theories in the communications developed within the framework, the general social theory are dealth with;the communication theories that basic underlying structure.

IISL308 - Managerial Communication (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

In this lesson, subjects such as basic conceps about communication, process and factors, functions of communicaiton, oral communication, importance of communication for managers, instruments used in comunicaiton, types of  communication, affective communication, communication media , effective presentation preparition technichs, futures  of target (listener ) and enviroment, communicaiton and target comminity and organizational communication are  explanied

IISL318 - Behavioural Finance (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

Understanding financial market anomalies, understanding the differences between neoclassical behavioral models and behavioral finance models, understanding the effects of uncertainty for decision making and market movements

IISL306 Tax Law (3-0) 3, 3 AKTS

The aim of the course is to provide the student with information concerning the understanding of the tax law at national and international level.

The aim of the course is to enable students to recognize the basic marketing concepts and modern marketing approaches.

IIYB302 Research Methods (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of the course is to give information about the bases of the concept 'research’ and the qualitative and quantitative scientific methods that use these bases.

7. SEMESTER

The aim of the course is to help students advance their professional English levels that they reached during YDL302.

IEKO401 Money Theory and Policy (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

To explain the theories about the money market and explain the functioning mechanisms of money, banking and financial markets.

IISL401 Consumer Behaviour (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The purpose of this course is based on the concepts of consumer behavior and marketing approaches of modern behavior, marketing practices affect the psychological, socio-cultural and demographic factors to teach.

IISL407 Management and Organization (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide students with basic information about the concept of management and management functions (planning, organising, leading, coordinating, controlling).

The main purpose of this course is to teach basic concepts about brand and brand management and it also aims to read students configuration models and communication strategies for brands. In this context, by starting meaning of brand for consumers, effects of brand on consumers, central role of brand in integrated communication efforts with historical context will be taken in hand.

18ISEC3 Elective - III

IISL415 - Quantitative Decision Making (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The main purposes of the study are to teach basic concepts related to decision making and fundemental methods in decision making process and to provide ability to use methods in decision making. Course is also provide skills to make decision under risky and unceratin environment.

IISL411 - Customer Relations Management (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The aim of this course is to give information about strategies for setting up profitable relationships with customers.

IISL419 - World Economy and Globalization (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

Aim of this course is to maintain an opinion about the current economic problems of World economics, to learn necessary concepts and informations in order to observe the developments in the world economy, to assess Turkey's  economic development within the World economy.

The aim of this course is to make the students have the ability on making an academic research as a thesis.

8. SEMESTER

IYDL402 Foreign Language-VI (3-0) 3, 3 AKTS

The aim of the course is to help students advance their professional English levels that they reached during YDL401.

IIYB402 Graduation Project (0-0) 0, 12 AKTS

The purpose of this course, according to the rules of scientific research subject selected as a final project, write a report in front of the jury and the defense of the project.

IISL420 Investment Analysis (3-0) 3, 5 AKTS

The aim of this course is to provide students with the decision-making methods and evaluation of the medium and long-term investments through feasibility studies.

IISL402 Strategic Management (3-0) 3, 6 AKTS

The aim of the course is to provide students with theoretical and practical knowledge and competency about making, implementation and control of long-term decisions and plans.

18ISEC4 Elective - IV

IISL412 - Financial Markets and Institutions (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The objective of this course is to analyze the role of financial institutions in allocating resources, managing risk, and  exerting corporate governance over firms. Special attention is given to the importance of financial regulation and its effects on the efficiency of financial markets.

IISL418 - Inovation Management (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

To provide the ability of using key tools and methods which is required to manage technology.

IISL414 - International Business Management (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

The objective of the course is to evaluate the different environmental aspects that international organizations face during their activities and to learn the strategies that multinationals refer while developing and sustaining their competitive advantage.

IISL416 - Managerial Leadership (3-0) 3, 4 AKTS

To analyze leadership behavior that has a strategic importance in organizations in the context of several leadership theories and approaches, to comprehend and to evaluate several issues about leadership behavior.

This content was updated on 04/09/2024.
